---
title: "MCAV DEP 2026 — Task 8 Deliverable Tables & Figures (GE-8936)"
author: "Matias Gomez-Corrales"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: yes
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: yes
    code_folding: hide
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(DT)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
```

---

# Overview

BioProject: **PRJNA1478717** | SRA Submission: **SUB16257907** | Run: GE-8936, NovaSeq X, June 10 2026

---

# 1. Sequencing QC Summary

```{r qc-data}
qc <- tribble(
  ~sample_id,                ~group,                                    ~reads_M, ~q30,  ~coverage,
  "4-Control",               "Juvenile – Control (26°C)",               78.4,     96.1,  26.2,
  "5-Control",               "Juvenile – Control (26°C)",               63.6,     95.9,  21.3,
  "6-Control",               "Juvenile – Control (26°C)",               65.0,     96.2,  21.7,
  "1-Plus-3",                "Juvenile – CBASS-1 (29°C)",               85.1,     96.1,  28.5,
  "8-Plus-3",                "Juvenile – CBASS-1 (29°C)",               67.3,     95.9,  22.5,
  "9-Plus-3",                "Juvenile – CBASS-1 (29°C)",               66.6,     95.9,  22.3,
  "2-Plus-6",                "Juvenile – CBASS-2 (32°C)",               67.7,     95.8,  22.6,
  "3-Plus-6",                "Juvenile – CBASS-2 (32°C)",               82.0,     95.5,  27.4,
  "7-Plus-6",                "Juvenile – CBASS-2 (32°C)",               67.2,     96.2,  22.5,
  "5-Control-replicate",     "Juvenile – Control (technical replicate)", 84.4,     96.2,  28.2,
  "8-Plus-3-replicate",      "Juvenile – CBASS-1 (technical replicate)",65.2,     96.4,  21.8,
  "7-Plus-6-replicate",      "Juvenile – CBASS-2 (technical replicate)",75.9,     96.3,  25.4,
  "MC19-01",                 "Broodstock colony",                        74.4,     96.1,  24.9,
  "MC19-04",                 "Broodstock colony",                        67.3,     96.2,  22.5,
  "MC19-10",                 "Broodstock colony",                        77.5,     96.2,  25.9,
  "MC19-18A",                "Broodstock colony",                        59.1,     96.1,  19.8,
  "MC19-18B",                "Broodstock colony",                        58.3,     95.9,  19.5,
  "MC20-01",                 "Broodstock colony",                        58.0,     96.1,  19.4,
  "MC19-23A",                "Broodstock colony",                        62.1,     96.2,  20.8,
  "MC19-23B",                "Broodstock colony",                        72.1,     96.1,  24.1,
  "MC19-29",                 "Broodstock colony",                        69.9,     96.2,  23.4,
  "MC19-31B",                "Broodstock colony",                        48.5,     95.9,  16.2,
  "MC19-34",                 "Broodstock colony",                        64.6,     96.1,  21.6,
  "MC24-01",                 "Broodstock colony",                        60.2,     96.3,  20.1,
  "MC24-02",                 "Broodstock colony",                        55.7,     96.2,  18.6,
  "MC19-18B-Sperm",          "Sperm sample",                             79.0,     96.2,  26.4,
  "MC24-01-Sperm",           "Sperm sample",                             71.2,     96.1,  23.8,
  "MC24-01-Sperm-replicate", "Sperm sample (technical replicate)",       88.1,     96.4,  29.5
) %>%
  mutate(
    broad_group = case_when(
      str_starts(group, "Juvenile")   ~ "Juvenile",
      str_starts(group, "Broodstock") ~ "Broodstock",
      str_starts(group, "Sperm")      ~ "Sperm"
    ),
    broad_group = factor(broad_group, levels = c("Juvenile", "Broodstock", "Sperm")),
    sample_id   = factor(sample_id, levels = sample_id)  # preserve row order
  )
```

## 1.1 Table

```{r qc-table}
qc %>%
  select(
    `Sample ID`         = sample_id,
    `Sample Group`      = group,
    `Reads after QC (M)` = reads_M,
    `% ≥Q30`            = q30,
    `Coverage (×)`      = coverage
  ) %>%
  datatable(
    caption  = "Table 1. Per-sample sequencing QC summary (fastp, GE-8936). Reads = R1+R2 after adapter trimming and quality filtering. Coverage = (filtered reads × 150 bp) / 448,367,954 bp (M. cavernosa; Rippé et al. 2021).",
    rownames = FALSE,
    options  = list(pageLength = 30, scrollX = TRUE, dom = "tip"),
    filter   = "top"
  )
```

## 1.2 Summary Statistics

```{r qc-stats}
qc %>%
  summarise(
    n                   = n(),
    `Mean reads (M)`    = round(mean(reads_M), 1),
    `SD reads (M)`      = round(sd(reads_M), 1),
    `Min reads (M)`     = min(reads_M),
    `Max reads (M)`     = max(reads_M),
    `Mean % ≥Q30`       = round(mean(q30), 2),
    `Mean coverage (×)` = round(mean(coverage), 1),
    `SD coverage (×)`   = round(sd(coverage), 1),
    `Min coverage (×)`  = min(coverage),
    `Max coverage (×)`  = max(coverage)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Statistic", values_to = "Value") %>%
  kable(caption = "Summary across all 28 samples") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
```

---

# 2. QC Figures

```{r colours}
grp_col <- c("Juvenile" = "#4682B4", "Broodstock" = "#2E8B57", "Sperm" = "#8B008B")
```

## 2.1 Coverage Depth

```{r fig-coverage, fig.width=9, fig.height=10, dpi=150}
ggplot(qc, aes(x = coverage, y = fct_rev(sample_id), fill = broad_group)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean(qc$coverage), linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.7) +
  annotate("text", x = mean(qc$coverage) + 0.3, y = 1.5,
           label = paste0("Mean: ", round(mean(qc$coverage), 1), "×"),
           hjust = 0, size = 3.2, colour = "grey30") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = grp_col) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +
  labs(title = "Figure 1. Estimated coverage depth per sample",
       x = "Coverage (×)", y = NULL, fill = "Group",
       caption = "Coverage = (filtered reads × 150 bp) / 448,367,954 bp (Rippé et al. 2021)") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
```

## 2.2 Filtered Reads

```{r fig-reads, fig.width=9, fig.height=10, dpi=150}
ggplot(qc, aes(x = reads_M, y = fct_rev(sample_id), fill = broad_group)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean(qc$reads_M), linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.7) +
  annotate("text", x = mean(qc$reads_M) + 0.5, y = 1.5,
           label = paste0("Mean: ", round(mean(qc$reads_M), 1), " M"),
           hjust = 0, size = 3.2, colour = "grey30") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = grp_col) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +
  labs(title = "Figure 2. Total filtered reads per sample (fastp)",
       x = "Reads after QC (millions, R1+R2)", y = NULL, fill = "Group") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
```

## 2.3 % Bases ≥Q30

```{r fig-q30, fig.width=11, fig.height=5.5, dpi=150}
ggplot(qc, aes(x = sample_id, y = q30, fill = broad_group)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = grp_col) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(95, 97)) +
  labs(title = "Figure 3. Base quality ≥Q30 per sample (post-filtering)",
       x = NULL, y = "% Bases ≥Q30", fill = "Group") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
```

---

# 3. SRA Accession Numbers

```{r sra-data}
sra <- tribble(
  ~sample_id,                ~group,                                    ~collection_date, ~biosample,      ~run_acc,       ~run_url,
  "4-Control",               "Juvenile – Control (26°C)",               "2026-05-13",     "SAMN60904239",  "RUN:50613740", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613740",
  "5-Control",               "Juvenile – Control (26°C)",               "2026-05-13",     "SAMN60904240",  "RUN:50613739", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613739",
  "6-Control",               "Juvenile – Control (26°C)",               "2026-05-13",     "SAMN60904241",  "RUN:50613728", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613728",
  "1-Plus-3",                "Juvenile – CBASS-1 (29°C)",               "2026-05-13",     "SAMN60904242",  "RUN:50613727", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613727",
  "8-Plus-3",                "Juvenile – CBASS-1 (29°C)",               "2026-05-13",     "SAMN60904243",  "RUN:50613726", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613726",
  "9-Plus-3",                "Juvenile – CBASS-1 (29°C)",               "2026-05-13",     "SAMN60904244",  "RUN:50613725", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613725",
  "2-Plus-6",                "Juvenile – CBASS-2 (32°C)",               "2026-05-13",     "SAMN60904245",  "RUN:50613724", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613724",
  "3-Plus-6",                "Juvenile – CBASS-2 (32°C)",               "2026-05-13",     "SAMN60904246",  "RUN:50613723", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613723",
  "7-Plus-6",                "Juvenile – CBASS-2 (32°C)",               "2026-05-13",     "SAMN60904247",  "RUN:50613722", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613722",
  "5-Control-replicate",     "Juvenile – Control (technical replicate)", "2026-05-13",     "SAMN60904248",  "RUN:50613721", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613721",
  "8-Plus-3-replicate",      "Juvenile – CBASS-1 (technical replicate)","2026-05-13",     "SAMN60904249",  "RUN:50613720", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613720",
  "7-Plus-6-replicate",      "Juvenile – CBASS-2 (technical replicate)","2026-05-13",     "SAMN60904250",  "RUN:50613719", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613719",
  "MC19-01",                 "Broodstock colony",                        "2026-02-25",     "SAMN60904251",  "RUN:50613718", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613718",
  "MC19-04",                 "Broodstock colony",                        "2026-02-25",     "SAMN60904252",  "RUN:50613717", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613717",
  "MC19-10",                 "Broodstock colony",                        "2026-02-25",     "SAMN60904253",  "RUN:50613716", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613716",
  "MC19-18A",                "Broodstock colony",                        "2026-02-25",     "SAMN60904254",  "RUN:50613715", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613715",
  "MC19-18B",                "Broodstock colony",                        "2026-02-25",     "SAMN60904255",  "RUN:50613714", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613714",
  "MC20-01",                 "Broodstock colony",                        "2026-02-25",     "SAMN60904256",  "RUN:50613713", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613713",
  "MC19-23A",                "Broodstock colony",                        "2026-02-25",     "SAMN60904257",  "RUN:50613738", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613738",
  "MC19-23B",                "Broodstock colony",                        "2026-02-25",     "SAMN60904258",  "RUN:50613737", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613737",
  "MC19-29",                 "Broodstock colony",                        "2026-02-25",     "SAMN60904259",  "RUN:50613736", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613736",
  "MC19-31B",                "Broodstock colony",                        "2026-02-25",     "SAMN60904260",  "RUN:50613735", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613735",
  "MC19-34",                 "Broodstock colony",                        "2026-02-25",     "SAMN60904261",  "RUN:50613734", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613734",
  "MC24-01",                 "Broodstock colony",                        "2026-02-25",     "SAMN60904262",  "RUN:50613733", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613733",
  "MC24-02",                 "Broodstock colony",                        "2026-02-25",     "SAMN60904263",  "RUN:50613732", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613732",
  "MC19-18B-Sperm",          "Sperm sample",                             "2025-08",        "SAMN60904264",  "RUN:50613731", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613731",
  "MC24-01-Sperm",           "Sperm sample",                             "2025-08",        "SAMN60904265",  "RUN:50613730", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613730",
  "MC24-01-Sperm-replicate", "Sperm sample (technical replicate)",       "2025-08",        "SAMN60904266",  "RUN:50613729", "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra/RUN:50613729"
)
```

## 3.1 Table

```{r sra-table}
sra %>%
  mutate(
    run_acc = paste0('<a href="', run_url, '" target="_blank">', run_acc, "</a>")
  ) %>%
  select(
    `Sample ID`          = sample_id,
    `Sample Group`       = group,
    `Collection Date`    = collection_date,
    `BioSample`          = biosample,
    `SRA Run Accession`  = run_acc
  ) %>%
  datatable(
    caption  = "Table 2. BioSample and SRA run accessions (BioProject PRJNA1478717, Submission SUB16257907, filed June 12 2026). Click accession to open on NCBI.",
    rownames = FALSE,
    escape   = FALSE,
    options  = list(pageLength = 30, scrollX = TRUE, dom = "tip"),
    filter   = "top"
  )
```

## 3.2 Counts by Group

```{r sra-counts}
sra %>%
  mutate(broad = case_when(
    str_starts(group, "Juvenile")   ~ "Juvenile",
    str_starts(group, "Broodstock") ~ "Broodstock",
    str_starts(group, "Sperm")      ~ "Sperm"
  )) %>%
  count(broad, name = "n") %>%
  rename(Group = broad) %>%
  kable(caption = "Samples per group") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
```

---

# 4. Session Info

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
